role: Senior Data Analytics and Visualization Engineer

evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Advanced-level dashboards and reports; managing security; participating in architecture and testing processes; data products testing expertise
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: JD emphasizes quality and architecture work alongside agile delivery and CI/CD familiarity, creating roughly balanced velocity and rigour signals.
evidence.domain_risk: Product owners collaboration; data architecture concepts; data warehouses and data lakes
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Role serves product team decisions without explicit financial or regulatory language, suggesting moderate risk where errors affect business decisions but not compliance.
evidence.data_team_maturity: Collaborating with product owners; designing data models; managing security; participating in architecture
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Data function exists with defined roles (analytics, product collaboration, architecture participation), but limited signals of multiple sub-teams or mature governance.
evidence.jd_authorship: Advanced-level dashboards and reports; designing data models, preparing and profiling data, analyzing business problems; participating in architecture and testing processes
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Mix of specific technical tasks (data modeling, profiling) alongside generic phrases like 'collaborating with product owners', indicating hiring manager input with some recruiter boilerplate.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Collaborating with product owners on requirements and demonstrations
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Primary audience is product team for requirement gathering and demonstrating analytics outputs.
evidence.autonomy_level: Collaborating with product owners; participating in architecture and testing processes
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Role receives scoped work from product owners and executes against their priorities, with limited strategic ownership framing.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools, ML infrastructure, or AI-related candidate expectations anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: data products testing expertise; participating in architecture and testing processes
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing appears as a skill requirement and process participation, not as something the candidate owns or defines.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: managing security; data products testing expertise; CI/CD knowledge
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Operational reliability is secondary concern focused on testing and security practices, not compliance or regulatory risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: designing data models; preparing and profiling data; building advanced-level dashboards
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Verbs emphasize building and designing within existing architecture rather than establishing from zero.
evidence.language_gate: Upper-Intermediate and higher English proficiency required
evidence.loss_aversion: Managing security; data products testing expertise; participating in architecture. Role focuses on operational reliability through testing and security management.
